message:
feat(core,abi): port /etc/netgroup triple parser to core::netgroup (bd-ng-epic)

Lifts parse_netgroup_triples (~65 lines of byte-slice parsing
including comment-stripping, whitespace tokenization, case-insensitive
group name match, and paren-bounded triple extraction with per-field
whitespace trim) out of unistd_abi.rs into a pure-safe
frankenlibc_core::netgroup module.

New core surface:
  pub struct NetgroupTriple { host, user, domain }   // owned Vec<u8>
  pub fn parse_netgroup_triples(content, group) -> Vec<NetgroupTriple>

The parser:
  - strips inline `#` comments
  - case-insensitively matches the requested group name
  - extracts each `(host,user,domain)` triple as it appears
  - trims per-field whitespace; missing trailing fields default to empty
  - returns gracefully on unclosed parens (stops scanning that line)
  - ignores bare group-reference tokens on the same line (matching
    the minimal glibc files-backend behavior — no recursive expansion)

abi changes:
  - Drops the local parse_netgroup_triples and NetgroupTriple struct.
  - NetgroupIterState now imports NetgroupTriple from core (public
    fields so the existing triple.host / triple.user / triple.domain
    accesses in getnetgrent / getnetgrent_r work unchanged).
  - setnetgrent calls frankenlibc_core::netgroup::parse_netgroup_triples.

The C-ABI marshalling — caller-buffer NUL packing for getnetgrent_r,
the (hostp, userp, domainp) out-pointers — stays at the boundary.

Verified:
  - 15 new core::netgroup unit tests pass
  - 2/2 netgroup unistd_abi integration tests pass

Closes bd-ng-epic.
